Through original programming, documentaries, and expert-led discussions, MHTN aims to increase awareness, provide valuable information, and encourage open conversations about mental well-being.

programme.title: Quo Vadis TV
programme.desc: Quo Vadis TV is committed to sharing the Biblical message in a way that makes Christian living practical, realistic, and attainable. Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

programme.title: Hope and Health With Mathew Embry
programme.desc: Angie Gensler is a mother and personal trainer who was diagnosed with multiple sclerosis in 2004. In this episode, Mathew Embry speaks with Angie about her diagnosis and why she ultimately chose not to pursue drug treatments. Instead, she focused on lifestyle changes including diet, exercise, and daily discipline. Along the way she faced several major challenges, all while continuing to pursue optimal health and eventually stepping onto the competitive bodybuilding stage in her 50s.

programme.desc: Weekends are for the Smack Down. Join Darrell Scott has he delivers tough talk on tough truths.

programme.title: Get Real

programme.title: Check the News
programme.desc: Jonathan Shuttlesworth gives hard hitting, no nonsense updates.

programme.title: The War Room
programme.desc: Steve Bannon and the team bring the most important news from around the world.

programme.title: Angel in My Pocket
programme.desc: After minister Sam Whitehead (Andy Griffith) moves to his new post in a small Kansas town, he finds that the place is in total gridlock because of a feud between the families of Mayor Will Sinclair (Henry Jones) and his rival Axel Gresham (Edgar Buchanan). Meanwhile, Sam's reckless brother-in-law, Bubba (Jerry Van Dyke), turns the church boiler into an alcohol still. Sam is ready to quit when his friend Art (Gary Collins) decides to enter politics in order to change the town for the better.

programme.title: Salome
programme.desc: Princess Salome (Rita Hayworth) is the daughter of King Herod (Charles Laughton) of Galilee. Cast out after her affair with Caesar's nephew, Salome finds herself back in the kingdom of her father when she falls in love with Claudius (Stewart Granger), the commander of her father's army. Meanwhile, Salome's evil mother, Queen Herodias, is continually being condemned by John the Baptist, and plotting to use Salome as a tool to get the prophet executed.

programme.title: Silver Lode
programme.desc: A Nevada rancher (John Payne) is taken from his bride (Lizabeth Scott) by a killer (Dan Duryea) posing as a U.S. marshal on the Fourth of July.

programme.title: Ten Wanted Men
programme.desc: Rancher John Stewart (Randolph Scott) prefers to resolve problems without falling back on violent solutions. However, fellow cattleman Wick Campbell (Richard Boone) isn't so peaceful, and he hires a team of armed mercenaries, led by ruthless Frank Scavo (Leo Gordon), to rid him of any possible competition. After narrowly surviving an attack from Scavo and his men, Stewart has little choice but to fight back in order to save both his ranch and his family, including nephew Howie (Skip Homeier).

programme.title: Seminole Uprising
programme.desc: An Army lieutenant (George Montgomery) is ordered to go after Florida Indians in Texas.

programme.title: The Man Behind the Gun
programme.desc: An Army major goes undercover to stop a dangerous plot to make Southern California a separate state, facing immense personal risk and complex challenges.

programme.title: Strange Affair
programme.desc: Eminent psychiatrist Dr. Brenner (Erwin Kalser) invites cartoonist Bill Harrison (Allyn Joslyn) and his wife, Jack (Evelyn Keyes), to a banquet honoring war refugees. Bill volunteers to pick up fellow psychiatrist Dr. Baumler at the train station, but the man vanishes when he has Bill stop so he can use a pay phone. At the dinner, Bill and Jack are seated with Brenner's daughter, Freda (Nina Foch), and, to Bill's surprise, another man is introduced as Baumler -- who dies moments later.

programme.title: A Face in the Crowd
programme.desc: Ambitious young radio producer Marcia Jeffries (Patricia Neal) finds a charming rogue named Larry "Lonesome" Rhodes (Andy Griffith) in an Arkansas drunk tank and puts him on the air. Soon, Rhodes' local popularity gets him an appearance on television in Memphis, which he parlays into national network stardom that he uses to endorse a presidential candidate for personal gain. But the increasingly petulant star's ego, arrogance and womanizing threaten his rise to the top.

programme.title: Hobson's Choice
programme.desc: Henry Hobson (Charles Laughton), a British widower, is the overbearing owner of a shoe shop. His three daughters -- Alice, Vicky and Maggie (Brenda De Banzie) -- work for him and all are eager to get out from under his thumb. When the headstrong Maggie announces she intends to marry Henry's best employee, Will (John Mills), father and daughter engage in an intense showdown. As Maggie works on launching a competing business, she also helps her sisters free themselves of their domineering father.

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Henrietta Doran is a rich American who's agreed to wed Lord Robert St. Simon, widely regarded as the most eligible bachelor in England.

programme.title: Rimfire
programme.desc: An undercover Army captain (James Millican) links missing gold and murder to a gambler's (Reed Hadley) ghost.

programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: Rio Lobo
programme.desc: Union leader Cord McNally is protecting a routine gold shipment when his troops are attacked by Confederate forces. Not only does he lose the gold, but one of his strongest officers is killed in the raid. At the end of the Civil War, McNally learns that the raiders had help from the inside, and he vows to uncover the two traitors. After a chance encounter with one of the turncoats, McNally travels to the town of Rio Lobo and makes an unexpected discovery.

programme.title: One Foot in Heaven
programme.desc: After hearing an evangelist preach, William Spence (Fredric March) abandons his dream of becoming a doctor in favor of studying for the clergy. Once ordained, he and his new wife, Hope (Martha Scott), begin a series of moves from one small-town church to another, each with a new set of challenges, not the least of which is the general orneriness of their parishioners. Along the way, they have three children, and William gradually becomes more flexible about church doctrine.

programme.title: The Story of Jacob and Joseph
programme.desc: Biblical dramas show Jacob (Keith Michell) and his twin, and Jacob's 11th son, Joseph (Tony Lo Bianco), who was sold by his brothers.

programme.title: Lightning Range
programme.desc: Over the course of a single day, a U.S. marshal (Buddy Roosevelt) navigates the trails of the Wild West and attempts to enforce the law. The bandit Black Pete (Olin Francis) makes it difficult for him. But the brave marshal forges on, facing violent outlaws, stagecoach holdups, scams, ambushes, arson and robberies. He even assumes the role of the groom in a hasty marriage ceremony for the lovely Dorothy Horton (Patsy Bellamy), who must be married by noon to claim her inheritance.

programme.desc: A dead young man's affair with a beautiful widow brings odd, dangerous events to his grandmother's house.

programme.title: Hellfighters
programme.desc: Chance Buckman (John Wayne) has one of the most dangerous jobs in the world. He, his assistant Greg (Jim Hutton) and his team of skilled firefighters extinguish raging oil field fires all over the world, cheating death every time. Madelyn (Vera Miles), his wife, is tired of worrying about Chance every time he leaves, and their marriage is beginning to fall apart. As Chance falls for Greg's daughter, Tish (Katharine Ross), he must battle both the flames in the field and the ones in his heart.

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: After swiping a loaf of bread to feed his family, Frenchman Jean Valjean (Richard Jordan) is put behind bars. After countless failed attempts, the poor man finally manages to escape from prison -- but not from the attentions of determined police officer Javert (Anthony Perkins). Years pass, and Valjean has made a new life for himself as the mayor of a French town. But his secret past comes back to haunt him when his old adversary suddenly makes an appearance.

Mary (Siobhan McKenna) steals away with her young son, Jesus, who grows up preaching, performing miracles and acquiring devotees. One of Jesus' (Jeffrey Hunter) followers, Judas (Rip Torn), betrays him, and he is sentenced to crucifixion. But Jesus has always known of his fate and has prepared himself for death.

programme.title: Fauda
programme.desc: After retiring from the Israel Defense Force, Doron is working on a vineyard. He gets roped back into active duty, though, upon learning that an enemy who Doron thought his unit had killed is still alive and plans on attending his brother's wedding. Doron rejoins the IDF team with the intention of going under cover at the wedding, but the plan goes awry when the team's cover is blown. That sets into motion a chaotic chain of events and leads IDF into a manhunt to locate and kill the squad's target.

programme.desc: Children are the future -- and so are their inventions, which is why "Think Big" could be an important show. It features young inventors competing to see who can come up with the most-innovative invention. Each episode features two teams in the Think Tank -- a studio filled with art supplies and construction materials -- who brainstorm and choose materials before sketching, designing and building a prototype to be presented to a judge, who determines a winner. The champions earn bragging rights and the coveted Genius Cup.

programme.title: America's Heartland
programme.desc: Much of the food Americans eat is produced by farmers and ranchers in the country's heartland, but many people don't know how it's produced. "America's Heartland" provides information about the people and processes responsible for the availability of food and fuel across the country and around the world. The show's reporters and producers tell stories in topics that include farm families, consumer issues, animal welfare and crop sustainability. Some episodes also take the show abroad to countries such as Egypt and Taiwan to show the impact American agriculture has on the global economy.

programme.title: Bloomberg Deals

programme.title: Bloomberg: Balance of Power
programme.desc: Focusing on the intersection of politics and global business.

programme.title: Bloomberg Businessweek Daily
programme.desc: Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec bring reporting from the magazine that helps global leaders stay ahead, plus insight on the people, companies and trends shaping today's complex economy.

programme.title: Bloomberg: The Close
programme.desc: Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld break down the day's top market-moving stories and trading action around the closing bell.

programme.title: Bloomberg: Balance of Power
programme.desc: Focusing on the intersection of politics and global business.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: The idea of flying cars has largely remained science fiction, until now; a new generation of electrified aircraft is attracting the attention of investors and venture capitalists around the world.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Governments and entrepreneurs race to commercialize the moon; the efforts could unlock a new, deep-space economy, but to return and stay requires the highest levels of innovation.

programme.desc: Bringing global investors everything you need to know as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders.

programme.desc: The definitive guide for global news audiences to get the complete picture on the story of the day, through probing interviews and nuanced conversations.

programme.title: My Classic Car
programme.desc: Host Dennis Gage upholds his "honor the timeless classics" catchphrase in this series, as he travels across North America to visit prestigious auto shows and events, spotlighting everything from muscle cars to museum pieces. The handlebar mustachioed Gage takes viewers along for the ride and keeps it entertaining with his unique perspective and enthusiasm for all things automotive.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey digs into building a brand-new bed for the '59 Apache right beside the old one!

programme.title: Sam's Garage
programme.desc: Master mechanic Sam Mahdavi shares the expertise he's gained from years in the shop; from engine swaps and supercharger installs to diagnostic testing and high-performance builds; Sam delivers straight talk and honest advice.

programme.title: Racers Unchained
programme.desc: Tony Kanaan and Paul Tracy discuss their careers in open-wheel racing; Kanaan, an Indianapolis 500 winner and IndyCar champion, serves as the team principal for Arrow McLaren while the two drivers review their experiences in the sport.

programme.title: 2026 DAKAR Rally
programme.desc: Part race and part survival contest, just finishing is a victory in itself. Spanning 14 days and 5,000 miles, this marathon off-road event is a test of endurance that rewards precision and punishes mistakes.

programme.title: A Foreign Affair
programme.desc: (1948) Billy Wilder's film stars Jean Arthur and Marlene Dietrich as the chalk-and-cheese women in post-war Berlin with John Lund as the man caught between them.

programme.title: Prince Valiant
programme.desc: (1954) Swashbuckling adventure. One of King Arthur's courtiers, the Viking Prince Valiant (Robert Wagner) uncovers the evil Sir Brack's (James Mason) plot to kill the king. Can he foil him?

programme.title: Von Ryan's Express
programme.desc: (1965) Second World War adventure. Frank Sinatra stars as the leader of Allied prisoners of war attempting to slip the clutches of their Italian captors. Co-starring Trevor Howard.

programme.title: Violent Night
programme.desc: (2022) A gang of violent home-invaders find out the hard way that you REALLY don't want to be on Santa's naughty list, in Tommy Wirkola's dark, violent Christmas comedy. With David Harbour.

programme.title: The Great Wall
programme.desc: (2017) Fantasy adventure starring Matt Damon and Willem Dafoe. In 11th-century China, Europeans seeking the secret of gunpowder are caught up in a war against alien monsters.

programme.title: Mister Lonely
programme.desc: (2007) Surreal drama. Troubled Michael Jackson and Marilyn Monroe imitators (Diego Luna and Samantha Morton) find love on a Scottish island. In English and French/subs. Strong language/sex.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: 3:10 to Yuma
programme.desc: (1957) Classic western with Glenn Ford and Van Heflin. The capture of a murderous stagecoach robber propels an impoverished local rancher on a perilous journey. Directed by Delmer Daves.

programme.title: The Tall Men
programme.desc: (1955) Western. Clark Gable plays a Civil War veteran who, with his brother (Cameron Mitchell), accepts the role of trail boss on a huge cattle drive for a crooked cattleman (Robert Ryan).

programme.title: They Rode West
programme.desc: (1954) Tough western. A cavalry doctor (Robert Francis) discovers a Native American tribe is dying of malaria, but his by-the-book captain (Phil Carey) refuses to allow their treatment.

programme.title: The Desert Fox
programme.desc: (1951) Drama. James Mason stars in Henry Hathaway's sympathetic portrait of Field Marshal Erwin Rommel in the final years of his life during the Second World War. With Jessica Tandy.

programme.title: Deep Impact
programme.desc: (1998) Elijah Wood and Morgan Freeman star in the sci-fi disaster movie about impending meteorite-related disaster, attempts to destroy it, and possibly some cataclysmic havoc.

programme.title: Jennifer's Body
programme.desc: (2009) Gory horror starring Megan Fox and Amanda Seyfried. A macabre ritual literally turns a cheerleader into a man-eater. Can her nerdy friend save the day?

programme.title: Dead Man's Shoes
programme.desc: (2004) Shane Meadows' thriller starring Paddy Considine, Gary Stretch and Toby Kebbell. An ex-soldier is bent on revenge against the drug dealers who cruelly tormented his younger brother.

programme.title: Let Him Go
programme.desc: (2020) Neo-western with Kevin Costner. After losing their only son, a retired sheriff and his wife try to save their former daughter-in-law from her abusive partner.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Jubal
programme.desc: (1956) Western with Glenn Ford, Ernest Borgnine, Rod Steiger and Valerie French. A cowboy is given a job by a rancher, sparking lethal jealousy in a colleague and desire in the boss's wife.

programme.title: Anzio
programme.desc: (1968) Epic Second World War drama starring Robert Mitchum. A reporter gets trapped behind enemy lines during the costly 1944 Allied invasion of Italy. With Peter Falk and Robert Ryan.

programme.title: Broken Arrow
programme.desc: (1950) Western starring James Stewart. A gold prospector, tired of reprisal killings between whites and Native Americans, offers a truce to the Apaches' leader. But tensions resurface.

programme.title: The Son of Robin Hood
programme.desc: (1958) Swashbuckling adventure starring June Laverick. Robin Hood is dead and his band of followers seek a worthy new leader. And they get one who exceeds all their expectations.

programme.title: The Eagle
programme.desc: (2010) Historical adventure with Channing Tatum and Jamie Bell. A Roman commander and his British slave embark on a risky mission to recover a military mascot lost in Scotland.

programme.title: Knock at the Cabin
programme.desc: (2023) M Night Shyamalan's jarringly intimate end-of-days horror with Dave Bautista. An armed group insist that a family of three must make an impossible life-or-death choice.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Ghost...
programme.desc: ...Protocol: (2011) Part four of the Tom Cruise action-thriller franchise. The Impossible Missions Force fight to clear their name, while also trying to avert nuclear war. Busy, busy, busy!

programme.title: First Love
programme.desc: (2019) Takashi Miike's gory, violent crime-thriller. Budding boxer Leo helps a woman who's been forced into sex work but gets more than he bargained for. In Japanese/subs.

programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: (2016) Toby Jones and Catherine Zeta-Jones lead a stellar cast in a reboot of the comedy classic. The hapless Home Guard platoon gets involved with D-Day. But there's a spy in their midst.

programme.title: Tora! Tora! Tora!
programme.desc: (1970) Oscar-winning drama portraying the bombing of Pearl Harbor, from both American and Japanese perspectives. With Joseph Cotten and So Yamamura.

programme.title: Annie
programme.desc: (2014) Jamie Foxx stars in this reboot of the 1982 musical. Orphan Annie is transported to post-2008 recession-hit New York, where she becomes the ward of a germaphobe running for mayor.

programme.title: Battleship
programme.desc: (2012) Sci-fi action-adventure starring Taylor Kitsch, Liam Neeson and Rihanna. When aliens land spacecraft in the Pacific Ocean, only the US and Japanese navies can save Earth.

programme.title: Last Night in Soho Interview...
programme.desc: ...Special: Director Edgar Wright and co-writer Krysty Wilson-Cairns discuss travelling through time and recreating the past to make the ingenious and atmospheric psychological thriller.

programme.title: Anna
programme.desc: (2019) Luc Besson's action-thriller about a young Russian assassin, with Sasha Luss. A scout offers Anna an opportunity to become a model in Paris, but can she escape her links to the KGB?

programme.title: Last Night in Soho
programme.desc: (2021) Edgar Wright's horror with Thomasin McKenzie, Anya Taylor-Joy and Matt Smith. A fashion student's transported back to Swinging-60s London. But nostalgia's not what it used to be...

programme.title: The Frighteners
programme.desc: (1996) Peter Jackson's comedy horror stars Michael J Fox as a psychic investigator in a town that has more ghouls and ghosties than you can shake a spirit stick at. But is he a fraud?
